rationale: Test-coverage improvement PR that rewrites tautological tests into stronger validator/boundary tests as the body describes. `pyproject.toml` adds pytest `filterwarnings` to ignore Pydantic deprecation noise. New `tests/test_aircraft_data_hierarchy/test_behavior.py` (~190 lines) covers `behavior.py` branches with real assertions. New `test_airframe_geometry.py` provides parametrized boundary tests for geometry validators. The rewritten `test_airframe_parameters.py` (~770 lines reduced to 516) uses 24 `@pytest.mark.parametrize` decorators and 35 `pytest.raises(ValidationError, match=...)` calls with specific message matching, replacing input==output assertions. `test_systems_diagrams.py` mocks `graphviz.Digraph.render` (graphviz is a real mainstream Python package, not a hallucination — the test also asserts `isinstance(dot, graphviz.Digraph)` so the symbol is exercised) and uses `patch.object(_diag_mod, 'display' / 'Image' / 'HTML')` to avoid namespace collisions caused by `from .X import *` re-exports — exactly the fixup the PR body describes. Tests directory reorganized to mirror source layout. The `mock-of-hallucination` flag is a false positive: graphviz and IPython.display are both real, widely-used Python libraries, and the patch targets match real symbols in the imported modules.
